mnemonics: IRAC: If (Condition) Then (Action) for Clear rules
P.R.O.P.S. for good rule Properties: Precise, Readable, Operational, Purposeful, Specific
D.R.O.P. a rule to avoid mistakes: Don't Rule Overly complex Premises
keyPoints: Rules use an IF-THEN structure to represent knowledge as conditional statements.
A rule-based system consists of a knowledge base (rules) and an inference engine that applies them.
Forward chaining is data-driven, while backward chaining is goal-driven.
commonMistakes: Creating rules that are too broad or have overlapping conditions, leading to conflicts.
Confusing the direction of inference (e.g., using forward chaining when backward is more efficient).
Neglecting to ensure the rule set is complete and accounts for all possible scenarios.
